channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: How aid cuts are hampering the frontline response to the Ebola crisis
item.description: Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.
item.content:encoded:
Aid workers in Uganda are watching the Ebola crisis unfold in the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo. They're doing what they can to prepare for an uptick in cases, but foreign aid cuts aren't helping.

(Image credit: Leonard Musinguzi)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: The terrifying day the U.S.-Iran war reached a sleepy Sri Lankan town
item.description: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.content:encoded: A deadly strike during the first days of the Iran war hit far away in the Indian Ocean, jolting a quiet seaside town and showing how far the conflict's reach extends.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Trump floats MAGA rally instead of concert after musicians drop out of Freedom 250
item.description: Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded:
Several artists, including country singer Martina McBride, have withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington, D.C.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: United Airlines flight to Spain pulls U-turn, apparently over Bluetooth device name
item.description: The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.
item.content:encoded:
The flight to Palma de Mallorca diverted back to Newark late on Saturday. Air traffic audio and social media posts indicate an onboard Bluetooth device raised security concerns.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in Pacific Ocean, in fourth attack this week
item.description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Pacific Ocean, killing three in the fourth attack this week and putting the total death toll at 205.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military said it carried out another strike on a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Pacific Ocean, killing three in the fourth attack this week and putting the total death toll at 205.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel seizes medieval castle as it expands major offensive in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.
item.content:encoded: Israel has captured the 12th-century Beaufort castle that overlooks southern Lebanon and northern Israel. The move suggests Israel is planning an extended military presence in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
item.description: A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.
item.content:encoded:
A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.

(Image credit: Min Yu/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel is extending its footprint through war. For some, it's part of a greater plan
item.description: On the fringes of Israel's far right, some activists and political leaders dream of a Greater Israel, extending the country's area of control into neighboring countries.
item.content:encoded: On the fringes of Israel's far right, some activists and political leaders dream of a Greater Israel, extending the country's area of control into neighboring countries.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: The biggest permanent desert lake threatens with rising waters and hungry crocs
item.description: Kenya's Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake. Its waters have long sustained hundreds of thousands. Now the lake is facing multiple threats — and threatening those who rely on it.
item.content:encoded:
Kenya's Lake Turkana is the world's largest permanent desert lake. Its waters have long sustained hundreds of thousands. Now the lake is facing multiple threats — and threatening those who rely on it.

(Image credit: Tommy Trenchard for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Text and photos by Tommy Trenchard

item.title: Democrats and Republicans think control of Congress runs through Iowa
item.description: Iowa is no stranger to political attention in presidential elections, but surprisingly competitive midterm contests highlight the state's importance for both parties as it holds its primary Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Iowa is no stranger to political attention in presidential elections, but surprisingly competitive midterm contests highlight the state's importance for both parties as it holds its primary Tuesday.

(Image credit: Stephen Fowler | NPR)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
